5H/5H,SD

SPEED/ CONSUMPTION:



BALLAST/LADEN ABT 11.8 KN/ABT 11.5KN ON ABT 22.00MT/ABT 22.00MT IFO 380CST

(RMG 380)+ABT 0.10MT MDO



PORT CONS:IDLE ABT 3.00MT IFO 380CST(RMG 380)+ ABT 0.10MT MDO OR WORKING ABT

4.0MT BSS 12H OR 5.0MT BSS 24H IFO 380CST(RMG 380)+ABT 0.10MT MDO



ADDITIONAL ABT 0.5MT IFO 380CST(RMG 380) FOR IDLE OR WORKING IF THE AIR

TEMPERATURE IS UNDER 15 CENTIGRADE(INCL)



SPEED/CONSUMPTION BASED ON CLEAN BOTTOM $B!$(BWITHOUT DECK CARGO AND GOOD

WEATHER CONDITIONS WHICH MEANS:WINDS UP TO AND INCLUDING BEAUFORT SCALE 4 /

DOUGLAS SEA STATE 3(MAXIMUM SIGNIFICANT WAVE HEIGHT 1.25M) AND NO AFFECT OF

SEA AND CURRENT AND NO INFLUENCE OF SWELL AND ADVERSE CURRENT."ABT" MEANS 0.

5 KN DECREASE IN SPEED & 5% INCREASE IN CONSUMPTION. FAVORABLE CURRENTS

ALWAYS WORK TO OWNERS$B!G(B ADVANTAGE. ALL PERFORMANCE ASSESSMENT ARE CALCULATED

ON A GOOD WEATHER DAY BASIS 24 CONSECUTIVE HOURS FROM NOON REPORT TO NOON

REPORT.NO EXTRAPOLATIONS CAN BE MADE FOR OTHER WEATHER/SEA CONDITIONS THAN

THOSE DESCRIBED ABOVE.



VESSEL MAY CONSUME EXTRA BUNKER WHEN EXCHANGE BALLAST WATER, HOLDS CLEANING,

IN/OUT PORT, IN VERY COLD OR VERY WARM WATER, DURING LIGHTENING OPERATION /

DOUBLE BANKING/BUNKERING OR OTHER OPERATIONS WHEN VESSEL NECESSARY TO HAVE

THE ENGINE STANDBY OR ADDITIONAL A/E AND BOILER.



VESSEL CONSUMES MGO IN MAIN ENGINE, WHEN MANOEUVERING / NAVIGATING IN

SHALLOW, NARROW, CONGESTED, RESTRICTED AND CONFINED WATERS / CANALS /

RIVERS, ENTERING / LEAVING PORTS/CANALS, WITHIN SECA AREA, AND MAY ALSO BURN

MGO WHEN STARTING/SWITCHING OFF AND RUNNING AUXILIARIES AT SEA OR PORT.



CHARTERERS SHALL ALSO ENSURE, PRIOR TO THE VESSEL ENTERING IN ANY OF THE

EMISSION CONTROL AREAS (ECAS),TO SUPPLY THE VESSEL WITH SUFFICIENT QUANTITY

OF LOW SULPHUR BUNKERS (THE SULPHUR CONTENT PURSUANT TO THE REQUIREMENTS OF

ECAS) TO BE CONSUMED WHEN TRADING IN ECAS AREAS AT CHARTERERS RISK, TIME AND

EXPENSES WITHOUT MIXING THIS FUEL WITH OTHER BUNKERS ON BOARD.  IT IS

UNDERSTOOD THAT THE LOW SULPHUR BUNKER QUANTITY REQUIRED FOR CHANGE-OVER

FROM HIGH TO LOW SULPHUR BUNKER PRIOR ENTERING IN ECAS TO BE FOR CHARTERERS'

ACCOUNT AS WELL. FOR THE PURPOSE OF THIS CLAUSE, "EMISSION CONTROL AREAS

(ECAS)" MEAN THE AREAS DESIGNATED UNDER MARPOL ANNEX VI AND/OR ANY OTHER

AREA WHERE A MAXIMUM FUEL SULPHUR CONTENT REQUIREMENT IS IMPOSED BY REGIONAL

AND/OR NATIONAL AUTHORITIES. THE CHARTERERS MAY GIVE WRITTEN INSTRUCTIONS TO

THE MASTER - WITH COPY TO OWNERS -FOR SLOW STEAMING, SUBJECT ALWAYS TO

MASTER$B!G(BS OBLIGATIONS IN RESPECT OF THE SAFETY OF THE CREW, VESSEL AND

CARGO, WHEREOF IN ANY CASE OWNERS NOT TO BE HELD LIABLE FOR ANY PERFORMANCE

SHORTFALL. FURTHERMORE, PROLONGED PERIODS OF SLOW STEAM, ARE SUBJECT TO

VESSEL$B!G(BS MAIN ENGINE MINIMUM RPM RESTRICTIONS AS IMPOSED BY ENGINE MAKERS

AND/OR ANY OTHER PARAMETERS WHICH MAY AFFECT THE CONDITION OF THE MAIN

ENGINE DUE TO CONTINUOUS RUNNING WITH LOW RPM. OWNERS/MASTER/CH.ENGINEER,

ARE ENTITLED TO REVERT TO VESSEL$B!G(BS SERVICE SPEED WHEN SAME IS DEEMED

NECESSARY FOR MAINTAINING ENGINES$B!G(B INTEGRITY AND VESSEL$B!G(BS  SAFETY.



FUEL OIL TO CONFORM WITH VLSFO-RMG380(MAX SULPHUR:0.5%) (ISO 8217:2017(E)

STANDARD) / LSMGO-DMA (EXCEPT PP)(ISO8217:2017 (E) STANDARD)(MAX SULPHUR :0.

1%). CHARTERTERS SHALL PROVIDE OWNERS/VESSEL BUNKER COQ IN COMPLIANCE WITH

ISO 8217:2017 WITH RELIABALE LABORTORY'S STAMP/SIGNATURE NOT LATER THAN 24

HOURS BEFORE COMMENTCEMENT OF BUNKERING, FAILING WHICH ALL CONSEQUENCES/LOSS

TO BE ON CHARTERERS' ACCOUNT.

ON A GOOD WEATHER DAY BASIS 24 CONSECUTIVE HOURS FROM NOON REPORT TO NOON

REPORT.NO EXTRAPOLATIONS CAN BE MADE FOR OTHER WEATHER/SEA CONDITIONS THAN

THOSE DESCRIBED ABOVE.



VESSEL MAY CONSUME EXTRA BUNKER WHEN EXCHANGE BALLAST WATER, HOLDS CLEANING,

IN/OUT PORT, IN VERY COLD OR VERY WARM WATER, DURING LIGHTENING OPERATION /

DOUBLE BANKING/BUNKERING OR OTHER OPERATIONS WHEN VESSEL NECESSARY TO HAVE

THE ENGINE STANDBY OR ADDITIONAL A/E AND BOILER.



VESSEL CONSUMES MGO IN MAIN ENGINE, WHEN MANOEUVERING / NAVIGATING IN

SHALLOW, NARROW, CONGESTED, RESTRICTED AND CONFINED WATERS / CANALS /

RIVERS, ENTERING / LEAVING PORTS/CANALS, WITHIN SECA AREA, AND MAY ALSO BURN

MGO WHEN STARTING/SWITCHING OFF AND RUNNING AUXILIARIES AT SEA OR PORT.



CHARTERERS SHALL ALSO ENSURE, PRIOR TO THE VESSEL ENTERING IN ANY OF THE

EMISSION CONTROL AREAS (ECAS),TO SUPPLY THE VESSEL WITH SUFFICIENT QUANTITY

OF LOW SULPHUR BUNKERS (THE SULPHUR CONTENT PURSUANT TO THE REQUIREMENTS OF

ECAS) TO BE CONSUMED WHEN TRADING IN ECAS AREAS AT CHARTERERS RISK, TIME AND

EXPENSES WITHOUT MIXING THIS FUEL WITH OTHER BUNKERS ON BOARD.  IT IS

UNDERSTOOD THAT THE LOW SULPHUR BUNKER QUANTITY REQUIRED FOR CHANGE-OVER

FROM HIGH TO LOW SULPHUR BUNKER PRIOR ENTERING IN ECAS TO BE FOR CHARTERERS'

ACCOUNT AS WELL. FOR THE PURPOSE OF THIS CLAUSE, "EMISSION CONTROL AREAS

(ECAS)" MEAN THE AREAS DESIGNATED UNDER MARPOL ANNEX VI AND/OR ANY OTHER

AREA WHERE A MAXIMUM FUEL SULPHUR CONTENT REQUIREMENT IS IMPOSED BY REGIONAL

AND/OR NATIONAL AUTHORITIES. THE CHARTERERS MAY GIVE WRITTEN INSTRUCTIONS TO

THE MASTER - WITH COPY TO OWNERS -FOR SLOW STEAMING, SUBJECT ALWAYS TO

SHORTFALL. FURTHERMORE, PROLONGED PERIODS OF SLOW STEAM, ARE SUBJECT TO

AND/OR ANY OTHER PARAMETERS WHICH MAY AFFECT THE CONDITION OF THE MAIN

The speed and consumption so described is on average basis taken over the

entire charter period, including ballast voyage(s), provided no deck cargo

on board and provided no underwater parts fouling due to vessel remaining at

or shifting (for any purpose) within a place, anchorage(s) and/or berth(s)

to another for less than continuous 48 hours should be treated as breaking

precedent to redelivery that Charterers to remain responsible for

inspecting/cleaning (at Charterers' time, risk and expense) the vessel's

under water parts and super polishing the propeller prior to redelivery.

Cleaning to be to the Owners' satisfaction while the cleaning company as

well as the equipment to be used to be approved by Owners. Performance is on

the basis of 24 consecutive hours available good weather. 

  

The speed and consumption parameters provided in this description are not

subject to review by charterers who are expressly prohibited from

instructing Master to proceed at a speed and/or with a consumption other

than as provided herein. 

All methods of assessing the vessel's performance during the charter, other

than the good weather method, are excluded. 

  

Vessel burns LSMGO whilst manoeuvring, also whilst in restricted or narrow

or confined waters or in waters with restricted visibility. 

  

Port consumption idle about 3 MT VLSIFO + 0.2 MT LSMGO per day. 

Port consumption in cold or very warm weather add 1 MT VLSIFO per day extra.

washing, closing/opening of hatches and bilge pumping. Charterers to

BALLAST CLAUSE FOR LOW SALINITY WATERS

1. BALLASTING TIME (FULL BALLAST EXCEPT C/HOLD NO.3): 22 HOURS (AT ABOUT 750 MT/HOUR).

DE-BALLASTING (FULL BALLAST EXCEPT C/HOLD NO.3): 15 HOURS (AT ABOUT 1,100 MT/HOUR) - EXCLUDING STRIPPING.

2. BALLASTING IN LOW SALINITY WATERS (BELOW 0.9 PSU).

THE APT IS USED AS "FRESH WATER MODULE" OPERATION WHEN BALLASTING IN A FRESH WATER PORT (SALINITY BELOW 0.9 PSU).

SHIP'S APT HAS TO RETAIN HIGH SALINITY WATER (BALLAST) SO TO ENABLE THE SYSTEM NEUTRALIZE THE LOW SALINITY INTAKE BALLAST.

THIS WILL NEED TO BE KNOWN BEFORE LOADING HER CARGO, SINCE INEVITABLY IT MAY LIMIT HER CARGO INTAKE AND AFFECT HER TRIM, DRAFT,     STABILITY, DRAFT LIMITATIONS ETC. AT DISCHARGING PORT.

THE QUANTITY OF HIGH SALINITY BALLAST IN APT DEPENDS ON THE PSU OF THE LOW SALINITY INTAKE BALLAST (AT DISCHARGING PORT).

THIS BALLAST IN APT COULD BECOME ZERO IF THE TRADE OF THE VESSEL GUARANTEES THAT THE WATER AT THE DISCHARGING PORT IS OF SALINITY OVER 0.9 PSU. SO WHEN THE VESSEL DISCHARGES HER CARGO AND TAKES BALLAST (AT WATER SALINITY OVER 0.9 PSU), THERE WILL BE NO NEED TO USE THE "FRESH WATER MODULE" OPERATION AND SO APT CAN BE EMPTY.

SHOULD THE ABOVE WILL INCUR, OWNERS TO BE KEPT HARMLESS OF ANY LIABILITY UNDER THE C/P WARRANTIES/CONDITIONS/DESCRIPTION IN RESPECT THEREOF
